title: Visualizing the Boundary of a Set A in a Metric Space
description: This diagram uses a set A and its complement to define its boundary, ∂A. The boundary consists of all points that are arbitrarily close to both A and its complement, Aᶜ. It is visually represented as the 'edge' or 'frontier' of the set.
relationships: Every neighborhood (open ball) around a boundary point x intersects both A and Aᶜ
The boundary ∂A is a closed set
